title: The Socratic Paradox
subtitle: An interactive cognitive journal for the examined life.

steps.title: Maieutics
steps.heading: The Art of Giving Birth to Insight
steps.body: Socrates called himself a midwife of ideas. Maieutics is the disciplined conversation that helps you discover what you already know, by stripping away assumptions and confronting the logic beneath your thoughts. This journal does not lecture you; it asks.
steps.quote: I know that I know nothing.

steps.title: Cognitive Distortions
steps.heading: The Traps of Automatic Thinking
steps.body: Our minds generate shortcuts. Sometimes those shortcuts become habits that color reality in darker, sharper hues than it deserves. By naming a distortion—catastrophizing, mind reading, all-or-nothing thinking—you gain the first distance required to question it.
steps.quote: The mind is its own place, and in itself can make a heaven of hell, a hell of heaven.

steps.title: The Socratic Loop
steps.heading: Thesis · Interrogation · Synthesis
steps.body: Every entry follows a four-step arc: you state a raw thought (thesis), answer probing questions (interrogation), tag the distortions that surfaced, and conclude with a rational synthesis. The loop is not a test; it is a ritual of clarity.
steps.quote: An unexamined thought is not worth keeping.

wizardIntro.title: Begin the Examination
wizardIntro.body: Write the thought as it first arrives. Do not edit it into respectability. The rougher the thesis, the more honest the dialogue that follows.
